_doc: Single source of truth for input shortcuts. TWO LANES, kept strictly separate. WIRE shortcuts (simple, two-step) are rewritten into the outbound Anthropic request by the proxy middleware (tools/HME/proxy/middleware/00a_shortcuts_rewriter.js) -- they become real API messages to the model. MULTI-STEP shortcuts are LOCAL-SESSION: the UserPromptSubmit hook (tools/HME/event_kernel/claude_adapter.js) blocks the literal prompt and the PTY bridge (tools/HME/scripts/hme-claude.py) replays each step into the LIVE Claude Code REPL. A multi-step step like /compact is a REPL-local slash command that never travels over the API; placing it under simple/two-step would send the literal text to the model and counterfeit the action. RULE: the proxy reads ONLY simple+two-step; the adapter+bridge read ONLY multi-step. Loader: tools/HME/proxy/shortcuts_config.js (Node), tools/HME/scripts/hme-claude.py (Python).

simple.s: refocus, stop polishing screws on a sinking ship

cc.description: Compact the live Claude Code session, then continue. /compact is a REPL-local command, never a wire message; the PTY bridge types each step into the live session with an idle-wait between them.

c&.description: Dynamic compact shortcut. A prompt like c&continue compacts the live Claude Code session, then types the text after c& as the next live prompt. The c& prefix is local-session only and never becomes an API message.
